Our Story

You're not failing.
The system is.

"I searched everywhere for something written for me — not a textbook, not a clinical guide. Just one parent helping another."

Over Chaos & Doubt started as a quiet observation — of the often unseen ways OCD shapes everyday life, not just for those experiencing it, but for the families who stand alongside them.

Everything here was built with parents in mind. The book, the community, the therapist directory — each piece designed to fill the gaps in understanding, in conversation, and in the kind of support that feels both informed and human.

The Book

Six chapters that walk beside you

Written by Bhavya Nagpal. Not a clinical manual — a companion for the parent already carrying quite a lot.

◆

Ch 1 — Understanding The Alarm

What OCD actually is, the OCD cycle, and why the brain won't just let it go.

◆

Ch 2 — Resetting The Alarm

When noticing isn't enough — what happens next and how to bring it home.

◆

Ch 3 — Learning Through The Noise

School, stressful transitions, the conversation you've been putting off.

◆

Ch 4 — Beyond The Noise

They are so much more than this. When helping starts hurting. Their sky, not their storm.

◆

Ch 5 — The Way Forward

You're carrying a lot. That counts. You don't have to do this alone.

◆

Ch 6 — Pause And Reflect

Tiny wins. Right now, I notice. Spaces to slow down and return to as things change.

₹ 899
Physical + Digital Bundle Free shipping · 3–5 days

UDIT Major Design Project 2026

Book
The Book

A companion for the road

Not a clinical manual. A book that walks beside you — through what OCD actually is, the questions you haven't found words for yet, and the part no one talks about enough: how hard this is for you too.

₹ 899
Physical + Digital · Free shipping
Six Chapters

What's inside

—

Chapter 1 · Understanding The Alarm

So, what's actually going on? The OCD cycle, why it doesn't always look the same, and why the brain won't just let it go.

—

Chapter 2 · Resetting The Alarm

When noticing isn't enough anymore. What happens next, bringing it home, and from the alarm to the answer.

—

Chapter 3 · Learning Through The Noise

It's not the work, it's the doubt. Stressful transitions, making school feel a little safer, the conversation you've been putting off.

—

Chapter 4 · Beyond The Noise

They are so much more than this. Showing up differently. When helping starts hurting. Their sky, not their storm.

—

Chapter 5 · The Way Forward

You're carrying a lot. That counts. You don't have to do this alone. The best parts are still being written.

—

Chapter 6 · Pause And Reflect

Tiny wins. Right now, I notice. Scribble it out. Quiet spaces to slow down and return to as things change.

Written, designed, and illustrated by Bhavya Nagpal as part of a Major Design Project at UDIT, 2026. Medical expert consultation by Ishita Bhambri.

★★★★★
"I read it in two days. For the first time in a year, I felt like I had a map."
— Meera K., parent of a 13-year-old with OCD

Calm
Quick Relief Toolkit

Small tools for hard moments

When intrusive thoughts arrive, having something physical in your child's hands can help interrupt the loop. These are parent-tested, India-available tools that actually work.

These are not treatments. They are grounding tools that can offer short-term relief. Always work alongside a qualified therapist for OCD.

🟠
Sensory

Squishy Stress Balls

Squeezing activates the nervous system's calming response. Helps with hand-focused anxiety and repetitive thought loops.

Shop on Amazon India →
🌀
Focus

Fidget Spinner

Rhythmic, repetitive motion gives the mind something neutral to anchor to. Effective during homework or high-anxiety moments.

Shop on Amazon India →
🎲
Multi-sensory

Fidget Cube

Six sides, six sensory inputs. Clicking, spinning, sliding — lets restless hands stay busy without distraction.

Shop on Amazon India →
🧱
Creative Focus

Building Blocks / Lego

Structured, goal-directed play is one of the best natural grounding tools. Redirects obsessive focus into something constructive.

Shop on Amazon India →
💬
Comfort

Affirmation Cushion

A soft, physical reminder of a grounding phrase. Particularly useful at bedtime when intrusive thoughts tend to intensify.

Shop on Amazon India →
📝
Visual Anchor

Affirmation Sticky Notes

Place grounding phrases in the places that trigger anxiety most — the door, the desk, the bathroom mirror.

Shop on Amazon India →
🧸
Comfort Object

Soft Comfort Toy

A named, familiar object to hold during an anxiety spike. Especially helpful for younger children still developing coping language.

Shop on Amazon India →
⏳
Tactile

Kinetic Sand / Play Sand

The slow, controlled texture of kinetic sand mimics mindfulness grounding. A great de-escalation tool for after school.

Shop on Amazon India →

💬 18 replies❤️ 31

💬 35 replies❤️ 62

I'm grieving the child I thought I had. Is that normal?

Find a Therapist

Specialists who truly know OCD

Not all therapists treat OCD with ERP — the gold-standard approach. These practitioners do. Clicking any card opens their Practo profile.

Please note: This directory links directly to Practo profiles so you can read reviews and book. Always verify credentials independently before engaging any professional.
